text: by as he awaited some more profound reaction in himself, awaited the deeper movement in his processors that had hit him the first time. This dance was familiar, but bore no resemblance to the one she’d performed in her shack; he’d seen this sort of movement at Kitty’s . Her body was moving for him, but she wasn’t dancing . This was closer to a maintenance bot mowing the grass in the park. Movement with purpose, but without feeling. The motions of an automaton . “No,” he said . She jumped, startled, and swung her eyes to him . “ What ?” “This isn’t what I want .” “What the hell do you mean?” The line between her brows returned; danger ahead . She threw her arms to the sides, palms up. “I’m dancing. That’s what you wanted !” “You are moving your parts — ” “Isn’t that what dancing is? Moving your body ?” Ronin pressed his lips into a tight line until she was done interrupting. “Dance like you did the night I first saw you .” “What do you mean? Like…the same dance ?” “It doesn’t matter if it’s the same. Just…” There were hundreds of thousands of words — if he only counted English — stored in his memory with full definitions, and he still couldn’t find the right ones to convey his meaning. “I don’t want you to dance like you’re on that stage. Dance like you would for yourself .” She stared at him with mild surprise and confusion. It seemed she’d stay that way indefinitely, until, after twenty-five seconds, she walked to the table, grabbed a chair, and dragged it to where she’d been standing . “ Fine .” The chair’s legs whined as she turned it so it was sideways to Ronin. Facing the counter, she settled her hands on the back of the chair, took another deep breath, and stilled . She tilted her head to the side, locking gazes with him, and twisted her hips. Her legs moved, their motion so graceful, so fluid, that she appeared to be walking on air. She tipped the chair onto one leg and spun it, off-balance, twice. When she stopped it’s motion, the seat faced Ronin . Sliding her fingers over its top, she moved around the chair and lay down over it, her hair brushing the floor. Her other hand traced a path from her neck down, avoiding the mound of her breasts as it ran over her ribs and down to her knee . She didn’t break eye contact as she drew her legs up one-at-a-time, pants sliding down to reveal shapely calves. Lowering her feet to the floor, she sat up, swinging a leg to straddle the chair . Sparks crackled over the surface of Ronin’s skin. Though she was fully clothed, Lara’s dance was the most sensual thing he’d ever seen. That his experience was limited in such matters should have tempered the realization, but made no difference. His lingering arousal rekindled, far stronger than before . Her hands fell to her knees, only to slide up again, this time into her hair. Elbows out to either side, she lifted her wild locks and undulated her stomach. Her breasts strained against the fabric of her sweater . How had he never noticed the similarities between dancing and sex ? She intensified the movements of her torso and, for a few seconds, thrashed like a caged animal. Rage and frustration flowed from her, and raw sexuality. She was a wild, untamable force, displaying her majesty just for him . Ronin’s awareness of the room around him — of the house, the district, the town, the entire world — faded away, all his attention focused solely on Lara . And she somehow kept her gaze on him throughout, her eyes burning with silent, sultry intensity like he’d never seen. Standing, she spun and kicked the chair away. Her body moved like a whirlwind and a stalking cat at once, a contradiction, an impossibility, and he could not look away . She fell to the floor like she’d been beaten, only to surge up again, stronger, and repeated the movements over and over, varying them each time. She closed her eyes, her expression full of feeling . It was a message to him. He knew it, though he did not yet understand the language of her body. She was ocean waves crashing against the shore; relentless, powerful, and fluid . Synths were capable of more speed, of more precision — Lara never performed a move the same way twice — but her flexibility, her fluidity, and the way she threw herself off balance but never lost it combined into something impossible for a bot. Ronin should have known exactly how she accomplished it . Comprehension remained elusive, however; could it be her emotions? They were more visible with each passing moment, written on her face, conveyed through the building drama of her dance . Had he been arrogant enough to assume he’d puzzle it all out after a dance or two? Emotion was the key, it had to be, and that only raised more questions . Lara dropped to her knees, head bowed, arms hanging to the sides. Her disheveled hair obscured her face. The refrigerator buzzed, oblivious to the rhythm of her quick, ragged breaths . Finally, she lifted her face, tugging the hair out of it. Her eyes sparkled above flushed cheeks . “That better?” she asked . “Yes. Thank you .” Her eyes narrowed. “You didn’t like it .” There was danger in her statement, but he couldn’t determine why . “It’s given me much to consider.” He liked it very much, but needed time to determine the reasons . “The fuck does that mean?” She stood, breathing heavily. His attention was drawn to her chest again . “You’ve fulfilled your obligation tonight. Will you be ready again tomorrow ?” “It’s not like I need charging or anything .”
